name: Koloa Gulch
tagline: A rugged stream-walking adventure to a hidden waterfall deep in the windward mountains.
parking: Limited roadside parking on Kamehameha Highway near the trailhead — be respectful of residential areas
bestTime: Dry conditions are critical — never attempt after heavy rain due to flash flood risk
talkStory: This remote gulch was used by native Hawaiians for gathering medicinal plants — the steep walls create a sheltered microclimate with unique species. You're walking IN the stream for most of the 5-mile trail, making 20+ crossings over slippery boulders. The multi-tiered waterfall at the end drops into a narrow volcanic amphitheater few people ever see. Flash floods have killed hikers here — always check weather and never attempt after rain.
